tossups.question: This person is enveloped by two large hands, one of which has an eye in its palm, in a painting that also depicts a dove spreading its wings over the Earth and Moon. In a painting divided into day and night sides, this person holds a flag displaying the painting's title, which means “tree of hope, remain strong.” A painting given along with a mirror to Mary Sklar depicts this person and Fulang-Chang. A painting that has been lost since 1955 depicts this person sitting between a tiny-headed, dynamite-studded giant, a papier-mâché (*) skeleton, and a brown Nayarit figure at the title Wounded Table. A painting in which blood drips from scissors onto a white dress shows a vein connecting the exposed hearts of two depictions of, for 10 points, what painter, whose other self-portraits often feature monkeys and her unibrow?
tossups.answer: Frida Kahlo [accept either underlined portion; or Magdalena Carmen Frida Kahlo y Calderón; accept Las Dos Fridas or The Two Fridas] (The painting in the first sentence is Marxism Will Give Health to the Sick.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Frida Kahlo [accept either underlined portion; or Magdalena Carmen Frida Kahlo y Calderon; accept Las Dos Fridas or The Two Fridas] (The painting in the first sentence is Marxism Will Give Health to the Sick.)
